title: Cat Shaking Head Excessively: Causes, Urgency, and Next Steps
meta_desc: Is your cat shaking their head? Discover common causes like ear mites or infections, and learn why this behavior usually requires a vet visit.
content_html:
What does excessive head shaking in cats mean?

When a cat shakes their head repeatedly, it is usually a sign of irritation, itchiness, or pain within the ear canal or on the ear flap. This behavior is an instinctive attempt to dislodge a perceived foreign object, fluid, or parasite. Common causes include ear mites, bacterial or yeast infections, inflammatory polyps, or foreign bodies like grass seeds that have become lodged in the ear canal.

Is it an emergency?

Urgency Level: Low. Head shaking is typically not a life-threatening emergency; however, it is a sign of significant discomfort that should be addressed by a veterinarian within 24 to 48 hours. If left untreated, the underlying cause can lead to a painful ear hematoma (a blood-filled swelling of the ear flap) or a more serious middle-ear infection. If your cat also shows a persistent head tilt or loss of balance, seek veterinary care more promptly.

Why a photo helps with triage

Taking a clear, well-lit photo of the inside of your cat's ear can be incredibly helpful for a triage professional. A photo allows a vet to see the type of discharge present—such as dark, crumbly debris often associated with mites, or moist, yellow discharge indicative of an infection. It also helps them assess the degree of redness and swelling, which can determine how quickly your pet needs to be seen.

faqs.question: Can I use human ear drops on my cat?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human medications or over-the-counter drops without a vet's approval, as some ingredients can be toxic to cats or damaging if the eardrum is ruptured.

faqs.question: Are ear mites contagious to my other pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, ear mites are highly contagious between cats and dogs. If one pet is diagnosed, all pets in the household usually require treatment.

faqs.question: How can I tell if my cat has an ear infection?
faqs.answer: Common signs include a foul odor from the ear, redness of the ear flap, discharge (black, yellow, or brown), and sensitivity when the head is touched.
